args.message: Thanks for the pitch! A couple quick questions before we go further. First—any accidents or damage history on this one? And second, at 104k miles, how's the maintenance been? Any major work done recently, like transmission, brakes, that sort of thing?

text: Thanks for the pitch! A couple quick questions before we go further. First—any accidents or damage history on this one? And second, at 104k miles, how's the maintenance been? Any major work done recently, like transmission, brakes, that sort of thing?

speaker: seller

args.message: Great questions! So on the accident history—this is a clean title, no accidents, no structural damage. Previous owner was super careful with it. As for maintenance, you're in good shape here. We just had the transmission serviced—fluid and filter change about 500 miles ago—and the whole drivetrain is running beautifully. Brakes are solid, still have good pad life left. At 104k, this Altima is right in that sweet spot where it's proven reliable but you've still got plenty of life in it. Really, these Nissans go to 200k easy if you treat them right, and this one's been treated right.

args.reason: I appreciate your willingness to negotiate, but I'm not comfortable moving forward without seeing the Carfax report first. You've had multiple opportunities to pull it up, and now there's a "technical issue." Combined with the fact that service records aren't original documents and you initially didn't have the Carfax ready, I'm getting a pattern here that makes me hesitant. I take sellers at their word on most things, but verification on title and accident history is non-negotiable for me—that's the basic due diligence. I'd rather walk than test drive and then discover something later. This just doesn't feel right at this point. Good luck with the car.
